The one proposal I need is a quote on repairing ALL sidewalks, pathways, curb repairs, all potential trip hazards throughout the community. Now that the roofing project is completed, we now need to focus on repairing all the broken or lifted pathways around each building. I would need the proposal by Monday at the latest as I have to create a PowerPoint presentation due Wednesday of next week.
 
LMK if you would be interested in bidding that work out. I know GQM had bid it out about 2 years back but I am sure areas are worse. This is also to include the wheelchair accessible ramps that lead to the buildings as many of them are not leveled making it dangerous for my residents who are wheelchair bound to safely move around.
 
LMK and thank you again for your contribution.

jobs.Additional_detail:
When we were unclogging toilets for a unit in this building, The techs ran a camera through the line and found tree roots growing into the pipe. Additionally, the pipe appears to be broken. Although the blockage has been cleared for now, it is likely that the problem will reoccur due to the tree roots.
The pipe is shared between all the units. While we can use hydro jetting to temporarily clear the roots, this process will expose the holes in the line that need to be repaired. The roots extend about 10 feet under the slab at the front of the building. To properly fix the issue, we would need a permit, and the repair would involve excavating a 25-foot tunnel to expose the pipe under the slab. The pipe would then need to be replaced.

jobs.Additional_detail:
Good Morning both,
 
I need a proposal to have three of 6 plumbing lines for Bldg. 12 replaced which will include unit flooring replacement. Below is the information provided by the first company and the link to view the camera footage is below as I am not willing to pay any more camera fees from any other vendor since the footage is already available. I will need pricing from GQM on replacing 4 of 6 sewer lines ASAP please.
 
 
Subject: Camera inspection. Bldg. 12. 5 exits (They had previously checked the 6th line)
Observations:
On Friday, May 9, A camera was passed over the plumbing in Building 12, exactly on the plumbing lines leading to the apartment exits described below, in an attempt to detect the cause of blockages or other plumbing problems.
- In Unit 110/112, the line is backed up from the clean out (CO) to the connection to the unit's inlet. Approximately 40'. (Video attached)
- In Unit 109/111, the line is in good condition. (Video attached)
- In Unit 106/108, the line is backed up from the wall to approximately 10' inside the unit. (Video attached)
- In Unit 105/107, the line is backed up from the wall to approximately 25' inside the unit. (Video attached)
- In Unit 102/104, the line is backed up from the wall to approximately 20' inside the unit. (Video attached)
- Unit 101/103 line is in good condition (Video attached)
- The west main line is in a fall counter from 8' CO to 39' onward.
- The east main line is in a fall counter from 14' CO to 24' onward.
Recommendations:
- All plumbing lines that are backed up must be replaced and given the appropriate slope to prevent blockages in the lines due to buildup.
Below you will find the link to the videos.
https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1OwJpc5YGnbH9md5sz4fGssg4XkHKRWs-
jobs.Project_location: 911 S Park Rd, Hollywood, FL 33021, USA
